description: Reference of English loanwords commonly used in Nepali speech and writing, with phonological adaptation notes for TTS synthesis. These words must be pronounced with Nepali phonology, not English pronunciation.

notes.usage: Used by the TTS pipeline to ensure correct Nepali-phonology pronunciation of English-origin loanwords rather than code-switching to English pronunciation
notes.phonological_principles: Nepali adapts English loanwords by: (1) mapping English phonemes to nearest Nepali equivalents, (2) adding epenthetic vowels to break impermissible consonant clusters, (3) replacing English vowels with closest Nepali vowel qualities, (4) applying Nepali stress and intonation patterns
notes.orthographic_convention: Loanwords are written in Devanagari script following Nepali phonological conventions; spelling may vary across publications
notes.tts_relevance: The TTS system should use the Nepali romanization (not the English pronunciation) as the target for grapheme-to-phoneme conversion of these words

technology.phonological_adaptation: English /kəmˈpjuːtər/ adapted with full vowel /a/ replacing schwa, retroflex /ṭ/ for English /t/, final schwa added
technology.description: Universally used in Nepali; no native equivalent in common use

technology.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈmoʊbaɪl/ adapted with diphthong /ai/ preserved, final /l/ retained
technology.description: Extremely common; often used interchangeably with फोन to refer to mobile phones

technology.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈɪntərnet/ adapted with full vowel /i/ for /ɪ/, retroflex /ṭ/ for English /t/, schwa replaced by /a/
technology.description: Standard term for internet access; widely understood across demographics

technology.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈlæptɒp/ adapted with palatal onset /ly/ for /l/, vowel /a/ for /æ/, retroflex /ṭ/ for English /t/
English /ˈsɒftwɛər/ adapted with /a/ for /ɒ/, epenthetic vowel breaking /ftw/ cluster, /eyar/ for /ɛər/
technology.description: Used in technical and IT contexts

technology.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈhɑːrdwɛər/ adapted with short /a/ for long /ɑː/, /eyar/ for /ɛər/
technology.description: Used in technical and IT contexts alongside सफ्टवेयर

technology.phonological_adaptation: English /æp/ adapted with /e/ for /æ/, short monosyllable preserved
technology.description: Very common in daily speech; refers to mobile applications

technology.phonological_adaptation: English /foʊn/ adapted with aspirated /ph/ for English /f/ (Nepali lacks /f/), monophthong /o/ for diphthong /oʊ/
technology.description: Universally used; native Nepali दूरभाष exists but is rarely used in speech

technology.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈiːmeɪl/ adapted with short /i/ for long /iː/, monophthong /e/ for diphthong /eɪ/
English /ˈvɪdioʊ/ adapted with aspirated /bh/ for /v/, /i/ for /ɪ/, added /y/ glide, final /o/ for /oʊ/
technology.description: Very common; used for all video content

technology.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈwɛbsaɪt/ adapted with /e/ for /ɛ/, diphthong /ai/ for /aɪ/, retroflex /ṭ/ for final /t/
English /ˈsɜːrvər/ adapted with /a/ for /ɜː/, aspirated /bh/ for /v/, final /ar/ for /ər/
technology.description: Used in IT and networking contexts

technology.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈdeɪtə/ adapted with retroflex /ḍ/ for English /d/, long /a/ for /eɪ/, retroflex /ṭ/ for /t/
technology.description: Common in both technical and colloquial usage (e.g., mobile data)

technology.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈnɛtwɜːrk/ adapted with /e/ for /ɛ/, epenthetic /a/ breaking /tw/ cluster, /ark/ for /ɜːrk/
technology.description: Used for both computer networks and mobile networks

technology.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈprɪntər/ adapted with /i/ for /ɪ/, retroflex /ṭ/ for /t/, /ar/ for /ər/
English /ˈbraʊzər/ adapted with /j/ for /z/, /ar/ for /ər/, diphthong /au/ preserved
technology.description: Used in internet and computing contexts

technology.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈwaɪfaɪ/ adapted with /ph/ for /f/, diphthongs /ai/ preserved
technology.description: Extremely common in daily usage; refers to wireless internet

education.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈkɒlɪdʒ/ adapted with /a/ for /ɒ/, /e/ for /ɪ/, /j/ for /dʒ/
education.description: Very common; refers to post-secondary educational institutions

education.phonological_adaptation: English /juːnɪˈvɜːrsɪti/ adapted with /bh/ for /v/, /a/ for /ɜː/, all schwas replaced with full vowels
education.description: Standard term; विश्वविद्यालय is the Sanskrit-origin equivalent used in formal contexts

education.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈkæmpəs/ adapted with palatalized /ky/ onset, /a/ for /æ/, /a/ for schwa
education.description: Refers specifically to a college campus; sometimes used synonymously with college

education.phonological_adaptation: English /prəˈfɛsər/ adapted with /o/ for first schwa, /ph/ for /f/, /e/ for /ɛ/, /ar/ for /ər/
education.description: Used alongside native title प्राध्यापक; more colloquial

education.phonological_adaptation: English /əˈsaɪnmənt/ adapted with /e/ for initial schwa, /ai/ for /aɪ/, simplified cluster
education.description: Common in student speech; no widely used Nepali equivalent

education.phonological_adaptation: English /ɪɡˈzæm/ adapted with /i/ for /ɪ/, /j/ for /z/, palatalized /jy/ cluster, /a/ for /æ/
education.description: Colloquial alternative to परीक्षा, especially among younger speakers

education.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈlaɪbrəri/ adapted with diphthong /ai/ for /aɪ/, /e/ for schwa
English /skuːl/ adapted with short /u/ for long /uː/, initial cluster /sk/ preserved
education.description: Extremely common; विद्यालय is the formal Nepali equivalent but स्कुल dominates colloquial speech

transportation.description: Primary public transportation term; universally used

transportation.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈtæksi/ adapted with retroflex /ṭ/ and palatalized /ṭy/, /a/ for /æ/
transportation.description: Common in urban areas; refers to metered cabs

transportation.phonological_adaptation: English /trʌk/ adapted with retroflex /ṭr/ cluster, /a/ for /ʌ/
English /ˈmoʊtərsaɪkəl/ adapted with /o/ for /oʊ/, /a/ for schwas, /ai/ for /aɪ/
transportation.description: Often shortened to मोटर or बाइक in colloquial speech
Colloquial term for motorcycle; very common among younger speakers

transportation.phonological_adaptation: English /reɪl/ adapted with monophthong /e/ for diphthong /eɪ/
transportation.description: Used for railway/train; रेलगाडी for train (vehicle)
Standard term; विमानस्थल is the formal Nepali equivalent used in official contexts

transportation.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈtɪkɪt/ adapted with retroflex /ṭ/, /i/ for /ɪ/, final /a/ epenthesis before /ṭ/
transportation.description: Universally used for transportation and event tickets

transportation.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈpɑːrkɪŋ/ adapted with short /a/ for long /ɑː/, /i/ for /ɪ/, anusvara for /ŋ/
English /ɡəˈrɑːʒ/ adapted with palatalized /gy/, /a/ for schwa, /e/ for /ɑː/, /j/ for /ʒ/
transportation.description: Refers to vehicle repair shops; common in urban areas

food_daily_life.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈbɪskɪt/ adapted with /i/ for /ɪ/, final consonant cluster simplified, /u/ epenthesis
food_daily_life.description: Very common snack term; widely available and universally recognized

food_daily_life.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈtʃɒklət/ adapted with /a/ for /ɒ/, schwa deletion in middle syllable, retroflex /ṭ/ for final /t/
English /keɪk/ adapted with monophthong /e/ for diphthong /eɪ/
food_daily_life.description: Common bakery item; widely available in urban Nepal

food_daily_life.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈkɒfi/ adapted with /a/ for /ɒ/, aspirated /ph/ for /f/
food_daily_life.description: Increasingly popular beverage term; Nepal also produces coffee

food_daily_life.phonological_adaptation: English /hoʊˈtɛl/ adapted with monophthong /o/ for /oʊ/, retroflex /ṭ/ for /t/
food_daily_life.description: In Nepali, होटल primarily means restaurant or eatery, not just lodging; a critical semantic shift from English

food_daily_life.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈrɛstərɒnt/ adapted with /e/ for /ɛ/, /u/ for schwa, simplified final cluster
food_daily_life.description: Used for more upscale dining establishments; distinguished from होटल in modern usage

food_daily_life.phonological_adaptation: English /brɛd/ adapted with /e/ for /ɛ/, retroflex /ḍ/ for /d/
food_daily_life.description: Refers to sliced/Western-style bread; distinct from native रोटी

food_daily_life.phonological_adaptation: English /tʃiːz/ adapted with short /i/ for long /iː/, /j/ for /z/
English /ˈnuːdəlz/ adapted with short /u/ for long /uː/, /a/ for schwa
food_daily_life.description: Very popular food item; चाउचाउ is the more colloquial Nepali term for instant noodles

food_daily_life.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈaɪs kriːm/ adapted with /ai/ for /aɪ/, compound compressed, short /i/ for long /iː/

government_bureaucracy.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈɒfɪs/ adapted with /a/ for /ɒ/, aspirated /ph/ for /f/, /i/ for /ɪ/
government_bureaucracy.description: Universally used; कार्यालय is the formal Nepali equivalent but अफिस dominates speech
Used alongside native मन्त्री; both are common

government_bureaucracy.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈbʌdʒɪt/ adapted with /a/ for /ʌ/, /j/ for /dʒ/, /e/ for /ɪ/, retroflex /ṭ/ for final /t/
government_bureaucracy.description: Standard term in financial and government contexts; no common Nepali alternative

government_bureaucracy.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈpɑːrləmənt/ adapted with short /a/ for /ɑː/, full vowels replacing schwas, /iy/ glide inserted
government_bureaucracy.description: Used alongside native संसद्; both are common in media

government_bureaucracy.phonological_adaptation: English /pəˈliːs/ adapted with /o/ for schwa, short /i/ for long /iː/
government_bureaucracy.description: Universally used; प्रहरी is the formal Nepali term used in official contexts

government_bureaucracy.phonological_adaptation: English /kəˈmɪʃən/ adapted with /a/ for schwa, /i/ for /ɪ/, /s/ for /ʃ/, /a/ for final schwa
government_bureaucracy.description: Used in government body names, e.g., निर्वाचन कमिसन

government_bureaucracy.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈkʌmpəni/ adapted with /a/ for /ʌ/, /a/ for schwa
English /ˈprɒdʒɛkt/ adapted with /o/ for /ɒ/, /j/ for /dʒ/, /e/ for /ɛ/

government_bureaucracy.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈsɜːrveɪ/ adapted with /a/ for /ɜː/, /bh/ for /v/, /e/ for /eɪ/

medical.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈhɒspɪtəl/ adapted with /a/ for /ɒ/, /i/ for /ɪ/, /a/ for schwa, retroflex /ṭ/ for /t/

medical.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈdɒktər/ adapted with retroflex /ḍ/, long /a/ for /ɒ/, /ar/ for /ər/

Standard term for nurses; no common Nepali alternative in everyday speech

medical.description: Very common medical term; सुई (needle) is sometimes used colloquially

medical.phonological_adaptation: English /ˌɒpəˈreɪʃən/ adapted with /a/ for /ɒ/, /a/ for schwa, /e/ for /eɪ/, /s/ for /ʃ/
medical.description: Standard term for surgical operations; शल्यक्रिया is the formal equivalent

medical.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈtæblɪt/ adapted with retroflex /ṭ/ and palatalized /ṭy/, /a/ for /æ/, /e/ for /ɪ/
medical.description: Used for medicine tablets; चक्की is a colloquial Nepali alternative

medical.phonological_adaptation: English /vækˈsiːn/ adapted with /bh/ for /v/, palatalized /bhy/, /a/ for /æ/, short /i/ for long /iː/
medical.description: Very common especially since COVID-19 pandemic; खोप is the native Nepali equivalent

medical.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈæmbjələns/ adapted with /e/ for /æ/, /u/ for schwa, simplified consonant clusters

medical.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈklɪnɪk/ adapted with /i/ for /ɪ/ in both syllables
medical.description: Used for small medical facilities and doctor's offices

medical.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈɒksɪdʒən/ adapted with /a/ for /ɒ/, /i/ for /ɪ/, /j/ for /dʒ/, /a/ for final schwa

sports_entertainment.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈkrɪkɪt/ adapted with /i/ for /ɪ/, /e/ for second /ɪ/, retroflex /ṭ/ for final /t/
sports_entertainment.description: Hugely popular sport in Nepal; Nepal has international cricket status

sports_entertainment.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈfʊtbɔːl/ adapted with aspirated /ph/ for /f/, /u/ for /ʊ/, retroflex /ṭ/ for /t/, /a/ for /ɔː/

sports_entertainment.phonological_adaptation: English /mætʃ/ adapted with palatalized /my/ onset, /a/ for /æ/
sports_entertainment.description: Very common sports term for competitive matches

sports_entertainment.phonological_adaptation: English /tiːm/ adapted with retroflex /ṭ/ for /t/, short /i/ for long /iː/

sports_entertainment.phonological_adaptation: English /ɡraʊnd/ adapted with /au/ for /aʊ/, retroflex /ḍ/ for final /d/

sports_entertainment.phonological_adaptation: English /ɡoʊl/ adapted with monophthong /o/ for diphthong /oʊ/

sports_entertainment.phonological_adaptation: English /ˈhɛroʊɪn/ adapted with /i/ for /ɛ/, monophthong /o/ for /oʊ/, /i/ for /ɪ/
